name: Liberian Fintech â€” Sample Compliance Violations
description: Realistic, presentation-ready compliance violations for a Liberian mobile-money / cross-border remittance application. Use this dataset to demo Koda's Auditor without making a live LLM call.

violations.description: The `customers.national_id` column stores Liberian National ID numbers in plaintext. CBL-MFS Section 4.2 requires every PII column to be encrypted at rest with AES-256 or stronger and the encryption key managed via an HSM or KMS service.
violations.evidence: customers.national_id VARCHAR(50) â€” no column-level encryption, no TDE, no application-layer crypto envelope.
violations.recommended_action: Migrate `national_id` to a `BYTEA` column populated by AES-256-GCM ciphertext. Rotate plaintext values during the migration window and drop the cleartext column. Store the data-key in Supabase Vault or AWS KMS; never alongside the ciphertext.

violations.description: The `transactions` table has no immutable audit log. CBL-MFS Section 5.1 and the Liberia AML Act require every state change on a transaction (create / approve / settle / reverse) to produce an append-only, tamper-evident record retained for 5+ years.
violations.regulation_ref: CBL-MFS-2023 Â§5.1, Liberia AML Act 2011 Â§17
violations.evidence: transactions table has UPDATE-able columns and no companion `transaction_audit_log` table; no triggers, no row-level history.
violations.recommended_action: Create `transaction_audit_log` keyed by `transaction_id` with `action`, `actor_id`, `changed_fields JSONB`, `prev_hash`, `row_hash`, `created_at`. Populate via a `BEFORE UPDATE` trigger and chain `prev_hash â†’ row_hash` so any row mutation breaks the chain. Replicate nightly to a write-once bucket for the 5-year retention requirement.

violations.description: Outbound transactions to non-Liberian wallets do not capture the corridor metadata required by ECOWAS Directive D/2020/07 (origin country, destination country, settlement currency, sender ID type, beneficiary ID type). Without these fields the daily ECOWAS regulatory report cannot be generated.
violations.recommended_action: Add `corridor` (origin/destination ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes), `settlement_currency`, `sender_id_type`, `beneficiary_id_type` to the transaction schema. Backfill from the user profile for in-flight transactions. Build a nightly export to the ECOWAS PSIF reporting endpoint.

violations.description: All wallets have an unbounded daily transaction ceiling. CBL-MFS Section 6.3 requires tiered KYC: Tier 1 (basic ID) â†’ $200/day, Tier 2 (full ID + address) â†’ $2,000/day, Tier 3 (enhanced due diligence) â†’ $10,000/day. Enforcement is missing both at write-time and in the daily reconciliation job.
violations.evidence: WalletPolicy has `daily_limit DECIMAL` initialized to NULL; no row-level CHECK constraint; transaction-create handler does not consult `customers.kyc_tier`.
violations.recommended_action: Add `kyc_tier ENUM('tier_1','tier_2','tier_3')` to customers. Hard-code the per-tier ceiling table in code (it's a regulator-set value, not user config). Reject `INSERT INTO transactions` over the ceiling with a Postgres CHECK + a service-layer validator. Surface the daily-spend remaining in the customer-facing API.

violations.description: There is no implemented API or admin workflow for honoring data subject access, rectification, or deletion requests as required by the Liberia Data Protection Act Â§14â€“17. Without this, the company cannot meet the 30-day legal response window.
violations.evidence: No /dsar route, no admin Supabase RPC, no documented runbook in the repo.
violations.recommended_action: Build a `/api/dsar` endpoint accepting `access`, `rectify`, `delete` request types, gated behind verified-identity auth. Admin UI to triage requests + fulfill within 30 days. Implement a soft-delete column on customers + a 30-day grace period before hard-delete to support the right to rectification.
